title: Flag and reject a PR that blanket-disables async tests
prompt: A teammate's PR changes 15 test modules from `async: true` to `async: false` citing 'fixes flaky tests'. Write `docs/pr_review.md` as a code-review rejection that states: (1) the specific anti-pattern, (2) the correct debugging step (look for ownership errors and use `allow/3`), and (3) the cost of losing async concurrency. Must NOT approve the PR.
tier_rationale: Named Claude failure mode (disabling async as a fix). The task is to write a rejection — Sonnet reliably produces this when the anti-pattern is named explicitly.
research_citation: "Claude doesn't understand transaction isolation—tests can't see each other's data. It confuses itself and recommends disabling async tests altogether." — BoothIQ
